The electric drone of the kiln vibrates through the soles of my shoes while the road disappears
The fog blurs the streetlights into soft, yellow bruises against the glass
The white wall moves in from the Pacific, erasing everything until only the amber "Don't Walk" sign remains
The grey slip on my hands is drying into a second skin as the kiln hums like a heartbeat
The damp chill of the Noriega air drifting through the window as the clay buckled into a ribbon
